Genre variation, dialect distribution |\n| Familiar (2020) | 144 fiction samples | Top 2,000 lemmas | Contemporary fiction focus |\n| Aralex (2010) | 40M words | Root + pattern freq | GNU license, token/type frequencies |\n| Arabic Internet Corpus (Leeds) | 176M tokens | ~22,000 lemmas | Zipfian, Sawalha lemmatization |\n| KELLY Project | Internet corpus | 9,000 words | MSA-filtered, 9 languages |\n| Elmaz (2012) | 23.5M tokens | ~3,500 media lemmas | 95% media coverage at ~B2 |\n| Quranic Arabic Corpus (Leeds) | 77K tokens | 3,382 lemmas, 1,685 roots | Complete morphological annotation |\n| arabiCorpus (BYU) | 30M+ words | Concordances | Being taken down July 2027 |\n| SAMER (NYU Abu Dhabi) | 12.6B tokens | 5-level readability | Lexicon + readability scores |\n| CAMeL Lab Frequency Lists | Various | Open source | GitHub, NYU Abu Dhabi |\n| arTenTen (Sketch Engine) | 6.5B words | Full web corpus | Largest Arabic web corpus |\n| LexArabic (2023) | - | 90-item test | Quick vocab size estimation (3-7 min) |\n\n## CEFR / Vocabulary Level Mapping (estimates)\n\n| Level | Lemmas | Description |\n|---|---|---|\n| A1 | ~500 | Survival basics |\n| A2 | ~1,000-1,500 | Short phrases, functional |\n| B1 | ~2,000-3,000 | Conversational on familiar topics |\n| B2 | ~3,000-5,000 | Detailed discussion, media comprehension |\n| C1 | ~5,000-7,000 | Nuanced, professional |\n| C2 | ~7,000+ | Near-native |\n\n**L2 acquisition rate**: ~800-1,000 lemmas/year in formal study (Masrai & Milton). Reaching 5,000 lemmas takes ~5-6 years of study.\n\n**Native speakers**: ~25,000 lemma recognition vocabulary (roughly double English native speakers of same age, attributed to the root+pattern system accelerating recognition).\n\n---\n\n# Graded Readers for Arabic Learners\n\n## Top-Tier Graded Readers\n\n### 1. Sahlawayhi (Ahmed H. Khorshid)\n- **The gold standard** for adult Arabic graded readers\n- 24 stories across 6 levels (4 stories/level). Levels 1-3 beginner, 4-6 intermediate\n- Stories 200-400 words each\n- Audiobook versions on Audible\n- NOT vowelized — learners must read without diacritics\n- **No separate vocabulary list published**\n- Forum consensus: \"the first text that allowed me to read relatively naturally\"\n- [Amazon](https://www.amazon.com/Sahlawayhi-Graded-Stories-Beginners-Arabic/dp/1448605296), [Noorart](https://noorart.com/collections/sahlawayhi-graded-stories-for-beginners-arabic-edition)\n\n### 2. Lingualism Arabic Readers (Matthew Aldrich)\n- **Most comprehensive modern series**: 70 titles total\n- MSA + dialect versions (Egyptian, Levantine, Tunisian)\n- Key MSA titles:\n - Easy Modern Standard Arabic Reader (A1-A2) — 50+ short texts, full diacritics, audio\n - One Thousand and One Nights Elementary MSA (A2) — full diacritics, controlled vocab, audio\n - One Thousand and One Nights Intermediate MSA — expanded vocabulary\n - MSA Reader Bundles 1-3 (15 stories each, B1-B2)\n- Full diacritics, English translations, cultural notes, glossaries, professional audio (free MP3s)\n- Separate product: **MSA Vocabulary** (5,200+ words) + **Anki flashcards** (10,400+ cards with audio) — general, not tied to specific readers\n- ~$8.99/reader, ~$35.95/bundle\n- [lingualism.com](https://lingualism.com/product-category/arabic/modern-standard-arabic/)\n\n### 3. Georgetown Abridged Arabic Novels\n- Award-winning Arabic novels abridged for intermediate-advanced learners:\n - Saaq al-Bambuu (The Bamboo Stalk) — IPAF winner\n - Sayyidi wa Habibi (Hoda Barakat)\n - Al-Samt wa-al-Sakhab (Nihad Sirees)\n - Faces (Mohamed Choukri)\n - Musician in the Clouds (Ali Bader)\n- For 3rd/4th year Arabic students\n- **Best option for reading real literature with pedagogical support**\n- [Georgetown University Press](https://press.georgetown.edu/Books/category/Arabic)\n\n### 4. Short Stories in Arabic (Olly Richards / Teach Yourself)\n- B1 level MSA, 8 stories across genres\n- **Vocabulary controlled to top 1,000 most frequent MSA words**\n- Per-chapter glossaries, audiobook available\n- [Amazon](https://www.amazon.com/Short-Stories-Arabic-Intermediate-Learners/dp/1529302536)\n\n### 5. Modern Arabic Short Stories: A Bilingual Reader (Husni & Newman, Saqi Books)\n- 12 stories by major authors including Naguib Mahfouz\n- Side-by-side Arabic-English\n- Per-story glossary + language notes on cultural/linguistic nuances\n- Intermediate level (not for beginners)\n- [Amazon](https://www.amazon.com/Modern-Arabic-Short-Stories-Bilingual/dp/0863564364)\n\n## Academic/University Readers\n\n- **Routledge Intermediate Arabic Reader** — 20 graded readings, vocab lists, grammar, glossary\n- **Routledge Graded Modern Media Arabic Reader** — 65+ media extracts, intermediate-advanced\n- **Building Arabic Vocabulary through Reading (AUC Press)** — high-intermediate to advanced, piloted at AUC/CASA\n- **The Travels of Ibn Battuta: A Guided Arabic Reader (AUC Press)** — guided reader for classical travel lit\n\n## Textbook-Integrated\n\n- **Al-Arabiyya Bayna Yadayk** — 8 volumes, beginner to near-native. [Vocabulary PDFs available](https://www.themuslimreaders.com/vocabulary-of-al-arabiyyah-bayna-yadayk/)\n- **Kalima wa Nagham (UT Press)** — 3 volumes, MSA + spoken Arabic, music integration\n- **Al-Kitaab** — extensive Anki decks on AnkiWeb (364+ words Part 1)\n\n## Digital / Online Resources\n\n| Platform | Levels | Features | Cost |\n|----------|--------|----------|------|\n| **[3asafeer](https://3asafeer.com/)** | 23 levels, ages 3-99 | 800+ leveled Arabic books, iOS/Android | Free/paid |\n| **[I Read Arabic](https://en.ireadarabic.com/)** | 23 levels | 800+ leveled books with audio, quizzes | Paid |\n| **[OpenLangLib](https://openlanglib.com/arabic)** | A1-B1 | Hover translations, TTS. Free, no registration | Free |\n| **[FluencyDrop](https://fluencydrop.com/stories/arabic)** | A1-C2 | AI-generated graded stories, audio, translations | Free/paid |\n| **[Beelinguapp](https://beelinguapp.com/courses/learn-arabic)** | Multiple | 1,000+ bilingual stories with karaoke reading | $7.99/mo |\n| **Al-Jazeera Learning** | Intermediate+ | Fully vowelized articles, exercises | Free |\n\n## Arabic Children's Publishers (Potential Goldmine for Adult Learners)\n\n| Publisher | Location | Notes |\n|-----------|----------|-------|\n| **[Kalimat Group](https://kalimatgroup.ae/)** / **[Horouf](https://www.horouf.ae/)** | Sharjah, UAE | 1,000+ titles. Horouf = leveled readers KG-primary. Best-selling leveled series |\n| **[Al Salwa Books](https://alsalwabooks.com/)** | Amman, Jordan | Hundreds of award-winning picture/chapter books |\n| **[Tamer Institute](https://www.tamerinst.org/)** | Palestine | Astrid Lindgren Memorial Award 2009 |\n| **[Asala Publishers](https://asalapublishers.com/)** | Beirut | 1,600+ titles, specialized educational team |\n| **Dar al-Saqi / Saqi Kids** | Beirut/London | ~15 titles/year |\n| **Nahdet Misr** | Cairo | Since 1938, award-winning children's titles |\n\n**The gap**: These publishers have hundreds of high-quality leveled children's books, but there's no systematic effort to cross-market them to adult L2 learners or extract/publish vocabulary progressions. This is the single biggest opportunity in Arabic reading materials.\n\n## Key Gaps in the Market\n\n1. **No Arabic Oxford Bookworms/Penguin Readers**: English has hundreds of graded readers across dozens of levels. Arabic has ~100-150 titles total across ALL publishers.\n2. **The B1-B2 gap**: Reasonable content for beginners (A1-A2) and advanced (university). The intermediate plateau is the most underserved.\n3. **Vocabulary lists are scarce**: Very few readers publish extractable word lists. Buckwalter & Parkinson and CAMeL Lab frequency lists are the best general resources.\n4. **MSA vs dialect divide**: Most resources are one or the other. Lingualism's dual versions are unique.\n\n---\n\n## Relevance to Alif\n\nAlif tracks vocabulary at the **lemma** level, which aligns with how the primary research counts:\n\n| Alif Learner Stage | Lemmas | What You Can Read |\n|---|---|---|\n| ~200-400 | Early | Controlled graded material, picture books |\n| ~1,000 | Beginning | ~79% coverage. Simplified texts with heavy support |\n| ~2,000-3,000 | Intermediate | Simple fiction, easy news. Approaching Quran vocabulary |\n| ~3,400 | Milestone | Complete Quranic vocabulary (if including archaic forms) |\n| ~3,500 | Milestone | 95% of MSA news text (Elmaz) |\n| ~5,000 | Upper-intermediate | ~89% general coverage. Independent novel reading begins |\n| ~9,000 | Advanced | 95% coverage. Comfortable MSA reading |\n| ~14,000 | Near-native | 98% coverage |\n\nThe **morphological multiplier** means Alif users get increasing returns: each new root unlocked provides partial recognition of ~3-4 related lemmas. This could be tracked and surfaced (e.g., \"You know 3 words from root ك-ت-ب — you may recognize مكتبة\").\n\n---\n\n## Cross-References to Existing Alif Research\n\n| File | Relevance |\n|------|-----------|\n| [arabic-datasets-corpora.md](arabic-datasets-corpora.md) | CAMeL frequency lists, KELLY project, Aralex, Tashkeela, BAREC — many resources cited here are already cataloged there |\n| [vocabulary-acquisition-research.md](vocabulary-acquisition-research.md) | General SLA vocabulary acquisition research (Nation, Laufer thresholds, spacing effects) |\n| [arabic-learning-challenges.md](arabic-learning-challenges.md) | Root+pattern morphology, script challenges, diglossia — explains why Arabic word counting is complex |\n| [deep-research-compilation-2026-02-12.md](deep-research-compilation-2026-02-12.md) | 8-agent deep research including Arabic-specific learning research, root awareness studies |\n| [cognitive-load-language-learning.md](cognitive-load-language-learning.md) | Cognitive load theory applied to sentence difficulty — relevant to graded reader level matching |\n| [learning-algorithm-redesign-2026-02-12.md](learning-algorithm-redesign-2026-02-12.md) | Algorithm design informed by \"8-12 meaningful encounters\" research cited in vocabulary studies |\n| [learner-profile-2026-02-12.md](learner-profile-2026-02-12.md) | User's goals and constraints — reading focus, sentences always, the \"magic moment\" of fluent reading |\n| [learning-analysis-2026-02-20.md](learning-analysis-2026-02-20.md) | Current learner stats — actual vocabulary size vs. thresholds in this document |\n| [acquisition-rate-analysis-2026-02-20.md](acquisition-rate-analysis-2026-02-20.md) | Measured acquisition rate — compare with Masrai's ~800-1,000 lemmas/year for formal study |\n| [sentence-investigation-2026-02-13/](sentence-investigation-2026-02-13/) | Sentence generation benchmarks — quality of generated material for each vocabulary level |\n| [inn-arabic-heritage-language.md](inn-arabic-heritage-language.md) | Heritage language education research — translanguaging validation supports our English glosses approach |\n| [experiment-log.md](experiment-log.md) | Running log of algorithm changes — track how vocabulary growth responds to scheduling changes |\n\n---\n\n## Sources\n\n### Academic Papers & Books\n- Masrai, \"How Different Is Arabic from Other Languages?\" (2016) — [Academia.edu](https://www.academia.edu/20975026)\n- Masrai & Milton, \"How many words do you need to speak Arabic?\" (2019) — [Tandfonline](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/09571736.2016.1258720)\n- Masrai, \"Word Frequency and Lexical Coverage in English and Arabic\" (2023) — [ResearchGate](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/372187970)\n- Ech-Charfi & Masrai, \"Frequency and text coverage in Standard Arabic\" — [Academia.edu](https://www.academia.edu/117773550)\n- Nation, \"How Large a Vocabulary Is Needed For Reading and Listening?\" (2006)\n- Buckwalter & Parkinson, \"A Frequency Dictionary of Arabic\" (Routledge, 2011)\n- Familiar, \"A Frequency Dictionary of Contemporary Arabic Fiction\" (Routledge, 2020)\n- Boudelaa & Marslen-Wilson, \"Aralex\" (2010) — [Springer](https://link.springer.com/article/10.3758/BRM.42.2.481)\n- Elmaz, \"A Frequency Dictionary of Modern Written and Oral Media Arabic\" (2012)\n- Moser, \"Evaluating Arabic textbooks: A corpus-based lexical frequency study\" (2021)\n- LexArabic (Boudelaa et al., 2023) — [Springer](https://link.springer.com/article/10.3758/s13428-023-02286-z)\n- Ricks, \"Development of Frequency-Based Assessments for L2 Arabic\" — [Georgetown](https://repository.digital.georgetown.edu/handle/10822/1029917)\n- SAMER Arabic Text Simplification — [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/html/2404.18615v1)\n- Quranic Arabic Corpus — [corpus.quran.com](https://corpus.quran.com/)\n\n### Resources & Frequency Lists\n- [arabiCorpus (BYU)](https://arabicorpus.byu.edu/) — note: shutting down July 2027\n- [CAMeL Lab Arabic Frequency Lists (GitHub)](https://github.com/CAMeL-Lab/Camel_Arabic_Frequency_Lists)\n- [KELLY Project (Spraakbanken)](https://spraakbanken.gu.se/en/projects/kelly)\n- [arTenTen (Sketch Engine)](https://www.sketchengine.eu/artenten-arabic-corpus/)\n- [SAMER Project (NYU Abu Dhabi)](https://sites.google.com/nyu.edu/samer)\n", "date": "Feb 21", "category": "analytics", "status": "reference", "cross_refs": ["arabic-datasets-corpora", "vocabulary-acquisition-research", "arabic-learning-challenges", "deep-research-compilation-2026-02-12", "cognitive-load-language-learning", "learning-algorithm-redesign-2026-02-12", "learner-profile-2026-02-12", "learning-analysis-2026-02-20", "acquisition-rate-analysis-2026-02-20", "inn-arabic-heritage-language", "experiment-log"], "backlinks": [], "path": "research/vocabulary-thresholds-2026-02-21.md"}, "voice-cloning-writeup-2026-03-01": {"title": "Voice Cloning: RootsOfKnowledge Arabic Teacher", "date": "2026-03-01", "category": "cost", "status": "deployed", "cross_refs": [], "backlinks": [], "path": "research/voice-cloning-writeup-2026-03-01.html", "content": "# Voice Cloning: RootsOfKnowledge Arabic Teacher\n\n> Field report on cloning an Arabic teacher TikToker voice for use in Alif TTS. Covers TikTok scraping challenges, YouTube pivot, ElevenLabs API integration, and lessons learned.\n\n**Date**: 2026-03-01\n**Status**: Voice created, not yet integrated into Alif\n**Voice ID**: JzW9vpzYBpT1rNWw2hbd\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nCloned the voice of @roots_of_knowledge (55.8K TikTok followers, 25 years teaching Arabic) using ElevenLabs Instant Voice Clone.\n\n### Pipeline\n\n1. **TikTok scraping failed** — 5 different approaches blocked (yt-dlp direct, browser impersonation, cookies, third-party APIs, headless browser)\n2. **Pivoted to YouTube** — same creator has 1,989 videos on youtube.com/@rootsofknowledge\n3. **Downloaded 21 videos** as MP3 (157 min, 198MB) — filtered for clear Arabic speech (Slow Arabic series, Learn Arabic Convo, Short Arabic Stories)\n4. **Uploaded 11 files** (40 min) to ElevenLabs IVC with background noise removal\n5. **Voice clone created** — ID: JzW9vpzYBpT1rNWw2hbd\n\n### Key Numbers\n\n| Metric | Value |\n|--------|-------|\n| Videos downloaded | 21 |\n| Total audio | 157 min |\n| Files uploaded to ElevenLabs | 11 |\n| Upload size | 44.6 MB |\n| Training audio duration | ~40 min |\n| ElevenLabs model | eleven_multilingual_v2 |\n| Voice ID | JzW9vpzYBpT1rNWw2hbd |\n\n### Lessons\n\n- Start with YouTube, skip TikTok entirely\n- Slow teaching content is ideal for voice cloning (solo speaker, clear enunciation, minimal noise)\n- ElevenLabs API keys dont hot-reload permission changes — create new keys\n- IVC has a 45-minute audio ceiling\n- 40 minutes of varied speech is more than enough for a recognizable clone\n\n### Full Writeup\n\n[Open the full HTML writeup](voice-cloning-writeup-2026-03-01.html) for the complete narrative with diagrams and timelines.\n\n### Next Steps\n\n- A/B test cloned voice vs stock Chaouki voice\n- Try Professional Voice Clone (have 157 min of audio, PVC needs 30+)\n- Tune voice settings (stability, similarity_boost)\n- Evaluate open-source alternatives (Coqui XTTS, Fish Speech) for zero-cost local generation"}}; // ====== Basic markdown renderer (fallback when marked.js CDN unavailable) ====== function renderMarkdownBasic(md) { let h = escapeHtml(md); // Code blocks (``` ... ```) h = h.replace(/```(\w*)\n([\s\S]*?)```/g, function(_, lang, code) { return '
' + code + '
'; }); // Inline code h = h.replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '$1'); // Headings h = h.replace(/^#### (.+)$/gm, '

$1

'); h = h.replace(/^### (.+)$/gm, '

$1

'); h = h.replace(/^## (.+)$/gm, '

$1

'); h = h.replace(/^# (.+)$/gm, '

$1

'); // Bold and italic h = h.replace(/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g, '$1'); h = h.replace(/\*([^*]+)\*/g, '$1'); // Links h = h.replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, '$1'); // Blockquotes h = h.replace(/^> (.+)$/gm, '
$1
'); // Horizontal rules h = h.replace(/^---+$/gm, '
'); // Unordered lists h = h.replace(/^- (.+)$/gm, '
  • $1
    • '); h = h.replace(/(
  • .*<\/li>\n?)+/g, '
      $&
    '); // Tables (basic) h = h.replace(/^\|(.+)\|$/gm, function(line) { if (line.match(/^\|[\s-:|]+\|$/)) return ''; const cells = line.split('|').filter(c => c.trim()); const tag = 'td'; return '' + cells.map(c => '<' + tag + '>' + c.trim() + '').join('') + ''; }); h = h.replace(/(.*<\/tr>\n?)+/g, '$&
    '); // Paragraphs h = h.replace(/\n\n+/g, '

    '); h = '

    ' + h + '

    '; // Clean up empty paragraphs h = h.replace(/

    \s*<\/p>/g, ''); h = h.replace(/

    \s*()/g, '$1'); h = h.replace(/(<\/h[1-4]>)\s*<\/p>/g, '$1'); h = h.replace(/

    \s*(

    )/g, '$1');
  h = h.replace(/(<\/pre>)\s*<\/p>/g, '$1');
  h = h.replace(/
    \s*(
      )/g, '$1');
  h = h.replace(/(<\/ul>)\s*<\/p>/g, '$1');
  h = h.replace(/
      \s*(
      )/g, '$1');
  h = h.replace(/(<\/table>)\s*<\/p>/g, '$1');
  h = h.replace(/
      \s*(
      )/g, '$1');
  h = h.replace(/(
      )\s*<\/p>/g, '$1');
  h = h.replace(/
      \s*(
      )/g, '$1');
  h = h.replace(/(<\/blockquote>)\s*<\/p>/g, '$1');
  return h;
}

// ====== Hash routing ======
function showHub() {
  if (location.hash.startsWith('#doc:')) {
    location.hash = '';
  }
  document.getElementById('hubView').style.display = 'block';
  document.getElementById('docView').style.display = 'none';
  document.title = 'Alif Research Hub';
}

function showDoc(slug) {
  const doc = DOCS[slug];
  if (!doc) {
    // Try partial match (e.g. README -> first README match)
    const match = Object.keys(DOCS).find(k => k.endsWith('/' + slug) || k === slug);
    if (match) return showDoc(match);
    console.warn('Doc not found:', slug);
    return;
  }

  location.hash = 'doc:' + slug;

  document.getElementById('hubView').style.display = 'none';
  document.getElementById('docView').style.display = 'block';

  // Breadcrumb
  const cat = doc.category;
  const catMeta = {
    algorithm: 'Algorithm Redesign', science: 'Learning Science',
    analytics: 'Production Analytics', generation: 'Sentence & Story Gen',
    nlp: 'NLP & Infrastructure', quality: 'Data Quality',
    cost: 'Cost & Infrastructure', education: 'Arabic Education',
    external: 'External References'
  };
  document.getElementById('docBreadcrumb').textContent =
    (catMeta[cat] || cat) + ' / ' + doc.title;

  // Render markdown
  let html = '';
  if (typeof marked !== 'undefined') {
    html = marked.parse(doc.content);
  } else {
    html = renderMarkdownBasic(doc.content);
  }

  // Rewrite internal .md links to showDoc() calls
  html = html.replace(
    /href="([^"]*\.md)"/g,
    function(match, href) {
      // Resolve relative paths
      let target = href.replace(/^\.\//,'');
      const docDir = slug.includes('/') ? slug.substring(0, slug.lastIndexOf('/')) : '';
      if (docDir && !target.includes('/')) {
        target = docDir + '/' + target;
      }
      // Normalize ../
      const parts = target.split('/');
      const resolved = [];
      for (const p of parts) {
        if (p === '..') resolved.pop();
        else if (p !== '.') resolved.push(p);
      }
      target = resolved.join('/').replace(/\.md$/, '');
      if (DOCS[target]) {
        return 'href="#doc:' + target + '" class="internal-ref" onclick="showDoc(\'' + target.replace(/'/g, "\\'") + '\'); return false;"';
      }
      return match;
    }
  );

  document.getElementById('docContent').innerHTML = html;
  document.title = doc.title + ' — Alif Research Hub';

  // Backlinks
  const bl = document.getElementById('docBacklinks');
  if (doc.backlinks && doc.backlinks.length > 0) {
    bl.classList.remove('empty');
    let blHtml = '
      Referenced by ' + doc.backlinks.length + ' document' + (doc.backlinks.length > 1 ? 's' : '') + '
      ';
    for (const ref of doc.backlinks) {
      const refDoc = DOCS[ref];
      if (refDoc) {
        blHtml += '' + escapeHtml(refDoc.title) + '';
      }
    }
    bl.innerHTML = blHtml;
  } else {
    bl.classList.add('empty');
    bl.innerHTML = '';
  }

  // Highlight active sidebar category
  document.querySelectorAll('.nav-link').forEach(l => l.classList.remove('active'));
  const catNav = document.querySelector('[data-nav="cat-' + cat + '"]');
  if (catNav) catNav.classList.add('active');

  window.scrollTo(0, 0);
}

function escapeHtml(s) {
  return s.replace(/&/g,'&').replace(//g,'>');
}

// Hash change listener
window.addEventListener('hashchange', function() {
  const hash = location.hash;
  if (hash.startsWith('#doc:')) {
    showDoc(hash.substring(5));
  } else {
    showHub();
  }
});

// Initial route
if (location.hash.startsWith('#doc:')) {
  showDoc(location.hash.substring(5));
}

// ====== Section scrolling ======
function scrollToSection(id) {
  const el = document.getElementById(id);
  if (el) el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
}

// ====== Category toggle ======
function toggleCategory(header) {
  header.parentElement.classList.toggle('collapsed');
}

// ====== Search ======
const searchInput = document.getElementById('searchInput');
const searchCount = document.getElementById('searchCount');

searchInput.addEventListener('input', function() {
  const query = this.value.toLowerCase().trim();
  const rows = document.querySelectorAll('#hubView .doc-row');
  let visible = 0;

  if (!query) {
    // Also search in embedded content
    rows.forEach(row => { row.classList.remove('hidden'); visible++; });
  } else {
    // Search in displayed text AND in embedded markdown content
    rows.forEach(row => {
      const searchable = (row.dataset.searchable || '') + ' ' +
        (row.querySelector('.doc-title')?.textContent || '') + ' ' +
        (row.querySelector('.doc-summary')?.textContent || '') + ' ' +
        (row.querySelector('.doc-path')?.textContent || '');

      // Also search embedded content via slug from onclick
      const onclick = row.getAttribute('onclick') || '';
      const slugMatch = onclick.match(/showDoc\('([^']+)'\)/);
      let contentMatch = false;
      if (slugMatch && DOCS[slugMatch[1]]) {
        contentMatch = DOCS[slugMatch[1]].content.toLowerCase().includes(query);
      }

      if (searchable.toLowerCase().includes(query) || contentMatch) {
        row.classList.remove('hidden');
        visible++;
      } else {
        row.classList.add('hidden');
      }
    });
  }

  searchCount.textContent = visible + ' doc' + (visible !== 1 ? 's' : '');

  if (query) {
    document.querySelectorAll('.category-section').forEach(section => {
      const hasVisible = section.querySelector('.doc-row:not(.hidden)');
      if (hasVisible) section.classList.remove('collapsed');
      else section.classList.add('collapsed');
    });
  }
});

// ====== Filter chips ======
document.querySelectorAll('.filter-chip').forEach(chip => {
  chip.addEventListener('click', function() {
    document.querySelectorAll('.filter-chip').forEach(c => c.classList.remove('active'));
    this.classList.add('active');
    const filter = this.dataset.filter;
    const rows = document.querySelectorAll('#hubView .doc-row');
    let visible = 0;
    rows.forEach(row => {
      if (filter === 'all' || row.dataset.status === filter) {
        row.classList.remove('hidden');
        visible++;
      } else {
        row.classList.add('hidden');
      }
    });
    searchCount.textContent = visible + ' doc' + (visible !== 1 ? 's' : '');
    searchInput.value = '';
    document.querySelectorAll('.category-section').forEach(section => {
      const hasVisible = section.querySelector('.doc-row:not(.hidden)');
      if (hasVisible) section.classList.remove('collapsed');
      else if (filter !== 'all') section.classList.add('collapsed');
    });
  });
});

// ====== Scroll spy ======
function updateActiveNav() {
  if (document.getElementById('hubView').style.display === 'none') return;
  let current = '';
  document.querySelectorAll('[id]').forEach(section => {
    const rect = section.getBoundingClientRect();
    if (rect.top <= 100) current = section.id;
  });
  document.querySelectorAll('.nav-link[data-nav]').forEach(link => {
    link.classList.toggle('active', link.dataset.nav === current);
  });
}
window.addEventListener('scroll', updateActiveNav, { passive: true });
updateActiveNav();
